mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate revenue contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and sustained increases in the consumer price index indicate stagflationary pressure, forcing investors to discount equity cash flows more heavily and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when retail sales decline and CPI rises concurrently, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and improving corporate capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings growth and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth fails to precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1100 thousand units signals severe weakness in residential construction and broader credit market stress, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where housing starts remain below the 1100 thousand unit threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor demand, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over 5-day forward returns across multiple expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 2.5% during periods of elevated inflation, market participants anticipate aggressive future tightening and real yield spikes, which compress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in over 50% of instances where the funds rate stays below 2.5% alongside rising CPI, invalidating the tightening anticipation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.
